mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term marginal predictive power is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of recovery regime windows, or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate exceeds 2.8%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during low-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through equity valuations.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with GDPC1 growth is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when coincident economic indicators remain stable.
